channel.title: CeruleanOak — From the workshop
channel.description: Devlog posts from CeruleanOak — one developer, small games.

item.title: v0.4 — Hut Interiors, Dungeons, Full Audio, and a Major AI Overhaul
item.description: Enemies have movement personalities now. Huts have real interior rooms. Each zone pulls from its own tiered roster that scales with depth. Zone-aware spawning means Green, Red, Cyan, Yellow, and Gray all feel distinct. Big one. [Pure Rogue]

item.title: v0.3, a First Boss, and Two Instruments
item.description: Dynamic music is live. Unlockable characters are in. Coded my first boss — it was immediately fun. Sound design constraint: square wave and noise only. It's somehow working. [Pure Rogue]

item.title: Got Distracted by DiceWars. Also Shipped Pure Rogue.
item.description: Fixed an old web game I've wasted too many hours on. Then shipped Pure Rogue v0.2 anyway. Turns out distraction and productivity aren't mutually exclusive. [Devlog]

item.title: Closing In on Full Migration
item.description: Collisions done, camera mostly done, saving and loading restored. Platforms and enemies are next — which is basically the game's entire variety. Getting close. [Penumbra]

item.title: The C++ Engine Runs Crisp (The Player Is Technically Present)
item.description: The bugs that kept haunting me in GameMaker are gone. The player is invisible — no sprites yet — but collisions work better than they ever did. Nearest-neighbor scaling is fixed. The framerate is genuinely exciting. [Penumbra]

item.title: Hanafuda Ships. GameMaker Gets a Farewell.
item.description: Shipped Hanafuda with a full visual rebuild and two new game modes. Then set up C++ and OpenGL from scratch. Totally normal November. [Devlog]

item.title: The Jury Is In on GameMaker
item.description: It's a great tool for experiments. It is not a great tool when things get complicated. The deliberations were brief. Time to transition. [Penumbra]

item.title: I Built My Own WaniKani Instead of Working on the Game
item.description: SMTP-powered quizzes, progress reports, scheduled emails — my personal server is becoming something. Stage two of the off-grid plan is underway. The game is also still happening. [Devlog]

item.title: Refactoring a 3,000-Line Camera and Regaining the Will to Live
item.description: The prototype runs. The code is more manageable now, though whether the refactor was worth it remains genuinely unclear. Also: I have a server again after 15 years. [Penumbra]

item.title: The Part Where I Decided to Make My Own Chrono Trigger
item.description: GameMaker prototype is running. Puzzle rooms, character switching, stealth detection, fake 3D elevation. 12-year-old me would be proud. I have a full-time job and three kids, which makes this either impressive or concerning. [Penumbra]
